title: narcissus cantabricus subsp. tananicus
intro: Narcissus cantabricus subsp. tananicus, also known as the Cantabrian Narcissus, is a subspecies of wild daffodil native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Narcissus cantabricus subsp. tananicus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
